AB Testing is an Optimization Problem

Simulations Show P-Hacking Is Good for Business

AB Testing
Bayesian
Decision Theory
Optimization
Author

Russ Zaliznyak

Published

May 30, 2026

In Progress

TL;DR

Significance testing is excellent at one thing: controlling error rates. If minimizing false positives is your goal, p < 0.05 is the right tool. But most product teams aren’t in the business of error control — they’re in the business of maximizing long-run value. That’s a different objective, and it calls for a different stopping rule.

We ran 100,000 simulated experiments across a range of baseline rates, post-test audiences, and effect distributions, scoring each stopping rule by the long-run conversion rate it produced — test traffic plus full post-test rollout. The findings are consistent across all scenarios.

The findings:

  • P-hacking is good for business.
    • Peeking at results early yields higher long-run value than waiting for a fixed sample size.
    • When an experiment ends inconclusive, deploying whoever is leading outperforms defaulting to control.
  • The optimal fixed-sample threshold is α = 0.50. Not 0.05. Not 0.10. Just deploy whichever variant is winning on the last day, every time. Conventional thresholds don’t leave a little value on the table — they actively cause you to default to control when the data already points elsewhere.

In all cases, the optimal stopping rule is far more aggressive than conventional practice — and any rule primarily designed to control error rates lands well short of the peak.

A Simulation Framework

Traditional power analysis starts from assumptions: fix α = 0.05 and β = 0.20, specify a minimum detectable effect, and it hands you a sample size. That workflow has two problems.

The MDE is a fiction. Power analysis requires you to commit to a single assumed effect — but effects follow a distribution. Some tests move the needle, most barely register, a few backfire.

It isn’t forward-looking. Power analysis tells you when to stop — not what the decision is worth once you ship to the full audience.

This framework addresses both. It simulates experiments across the full distribution of realistic effects and scores each stopping rule by the long-term value it produces — not the error rates it controls.

ⓘ Long-term value = total conversions generated across the full experiment lifecycle — both arms during the test period, plus the winner deployed to the post-test audience — divided by total users. A stopping rule scores higher by picking the true winner more often or by ending the experiment sooner, both of which put more users on the better variant. Marginal Events is the same metric expressed as incremental conversions over running no test.

Simulation Parameters

In order to determine optimal stopping rules, we simulated 100,000 experiments like the one below. Click any parameter to learn more.

The pre-experiment conversion rate. Sets the variance of your test statistic and determines how much room for improvement exists.
Total users in the experiment and their control/treatment split. The first 7 days are burn-in — no stopping decisions. The experiment runs at most 20 days.
The population exposed to the winner after the decision is made. More post-test traffic means more value at stake — potentially justifying a more aggressive stopping rule.
The realistic lift you expect comes from a distribution of possible effects — not a single number. Here, experiments have an equal chance of being good or bad.

Optimal Stopping Algorithm

To find the stopping rule that maximizes long-term value, we naively apply three analysis strategies and measure their average outcome across the same 100,000 simulated experiments.

Peeking Peeking + Best Fixed Sample
Stop early on signal ✓ ✓ ✗
No-breach fallback Retain Control Best Performer Retain Control

For each of 100,000 simulated experiments and each threshold α ∈ [0.01, 0.50]:

  1. Burn-in: No stopping decisions before min_test_days = 7
  2. Daily check: Stop when either condition’s Pr(Best) exceeds 1 - α*
  3. Rollout: Deploy the winner to remaining test users and post-test traffic
  4. Evaluate: Pick the α with the highest average long-term outcome

* Peeking strategies only. Fixed Sample skips the daily check and always runs to the final day.

Results

Below are the optimization curves for each strategy — long-term value as a function of stopping threshold α. The square marker is the peak.

  • Value vs α: Shows where each strategy is optimized. Compare peak heights to see which strategy produces more value; compare peak positions to see how aggressive the optimal threshold is.
  • Value vs Avg Trials: Shows the efficiency trade-off. A strategy that reaches the same value with fewer trials is strictly better — it frees up traffic sooner.

The table summarizes each strategy at its optimal α, with Fixed Sample at the conventional α = 0.05 included as a benchmark.

Change the scenario — notice whether the peaks shift or hold.
1.0s

Insights

ⓘ  Click a finding to highlight the relevant rows in the results table above.

Long story short: Significance testing protects you from being wrong. It doesn't optimize for being right. That caution has a measurable cost — value lost to experiments that run longer than needed, and value surrendered every time you default to control when the data already points elsewhere.

Importance of Post-Test N

The optimal stopping threshold isn’t fixed — it depends on how many users will see the winner after the test ends. Peeking+Best calibrates to that scale automatically. Fixed Sample can’t; its threshold is blind to post-test consequences entirely.

  • Peeking+Best automatically adjusts its optimal threshold as Post-Test N grows — requiring stronger evidence before stopping when more users are on the line.
  • Fixed Sample optimal α stays near 0.50 regardless of rollout scale. Stopping decisions are made at the end of the experiment, so post-test scale never enters the calculation.
  • Peeking is less sensitive to Post-Test N because its fallback is reverting to control — so the cost of a wrong call is already priced in regardless of rollout size. Peeking+Best's fallback is deploying the leader, which makes the accuracy of that final call matter more as post-test scale grows.

Code Snippets

Six reusable pieces from the analysis — the full pipeline from raw simulation through the test statistics that drive every stopping decision. All fully vectorized — no for loops.

create_simulation_df generates a DataFrame of daily binomial draws for one or more fixed-rate conditions. Each row is one day of one simulation.

import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days,
    number_simulations,
    condition_parameters,
    seed=None,
):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    # trials_per_day is the same for every day in the experiment
    n = np.array(
        [c["trials"] // number_test_days for c in condition_parameters]
    )[:, None]
    p = np.array([c["mean"] for c in condition_parameters])[:, None]

    rep = number_test_days * number_simulations
    n_mat = np.repeat(n, rep, axis=1)
    p_mat = np.repeat(p, rep, axis=1)

    # One binomial draw per (condition, day, simulation)
    samples = np.random.binomial(n=n_mat, p=p_mat)

    return pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "simulation": np.arange(rep) // number_test_days + 1,
            "day": np.arange(rep) % number_test_days + 1,
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_value": samples[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_trials": n_mat[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_true_mean": np.full(
                    rep, condition_parameters[i]["mean"]
                )
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
        }
    )

add_random_effect_condition appends a treatment arm where the true effect varies across simulations but is fixed within one. The effect is drawn from an ITC (Index-to-Control) distribution: ITC = 100 → no lift; ITC ~ N(100, 1) → effects centered at parity with modest spread.

import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def add_random_effect_condition(df, random_effect_parameters, seed=None):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    n_simulations = int(df["simulation"].max())
    n_days = int(df["day"].max())

    # One ITC draw per simulation — the unknown true effect for that experiment
    itc_samples = (
        np.random.normal(
            random_effect_parameters["itc_mean"],
            random_effect_parameters["itc_se"],
            n_simulations,
        )
        / 100
    )

    # treatment_mean = (itc / 100) x control_mean
    control_mean = float(df["condition_0_true_mean"][0])
    treatment_means = itc_samples * control_mean

    # Daily binomial draws; treatment_mean is constant across days within a simulation
    trials_per_day = random_effect_parameters["trials"] // n_days
    events = np.random.binomial(
        n=trials_per_day,
        p=treatment_means[:, None],
        size=(n_simulations, n_days),
    )

    # Reshape to tall format to match the existing DataFrame
    next_condition = (
        max(
            int(c.split("_")[1])
            for c in df.columns
            if c.startswith("condition_") and c.split("_")[1].isdigit()
        )
        + 1
    )
    return df.with_columns(
        [
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_value",
                events.flatten(),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_trials",
                np.full(n_simulations * n_days, trials_per_day),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_true_mean",
                np.repeat(treatment_means, n_days),
            ),
        ]
    )

add_cumulative_columns converts daily event counts into running totals grouped by simulation. Each condition gets a cumulative event count, trial count, and conversion rate. Cross-condition totals and remaining trials are derived in a single vectorized pass — no loops.

import polars as pl


def add_cumulative_columns(df):
    value_cols  = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_value")]
    trials_cols = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_trials")]

    # Running totals per condition, grouped by simulation
    cumsum_exprs = [
        pl.col(col).cum_sum().over("simulation").alias(f"{col}_cumsum")
        for col in value_cols + trials_cols
    ]
    df = df.with_columns(cumsum_exprs)

    # Cross-condition totals
    value_cumsums  = [f"{c}_cumsum" for c in value_cols]
    trials_cumsums = [f"{c}_cumsum" for c in trials_cols]
    df = df.with_columns([
        pl.sum_horizontal(value_cumsums).alias("total_value_cumsum"),
        pl.sum_horizontal(trials_cumsums).alias("total_trials_cumsum"),
    ])

    # Trials remaining in the experiment window
    df = df.with_columns([
        (pl.col("total_trials_cumsum").max().over("simulation")
         - pl.col("total_trials_cumsum")).alias("total_remaining_test_trials")
    ])

    # Cumulative conversion rate per condition
    mean_exprs = [
        (pl.col(f"{c}_cumsum") / pl.col(f"{c.replace('_value','_trials')}_cumsum"))
        .alias(f"{c.replace('_value','')}_mean_cumsum")
        for c in value_cols
        if f"{c.replace('_value','_trials')}" in trials_cols
    ]
    return df.with_columns(mean_exprs)

compute_cumulative_std_rates applies the binomial standard deviation formula sqrt(p * (1 - p)) to each condition's running conversion rate. Note: this is the std of the proportion, not the standard error — divide by sqrt(n) to get SE.

import polars as pl


def compute_cumulative_std_rates(df):
    mean_cumsum_cols = [col for col in df.columns if col.endswith("_mean_cumsum")]

    std_exprs = [
        (pl.col(col) * (1 - pl.col(col)))
        .sqrt()
        .alias(col.replace("_mean_cumsum", "_std_cumsum"))
        for col in mean_cumsum_cols
    ]
    return df.with_columns(std_exprs)

compute_delta_stats computes two things for each treatment condition vs control: the standard error (SE = std / sqrt(n)) and the delta statistics (delta_mean = mean_i - mean_0, delta_se = sqrt(SE_i² + SE_0²)). These are the direct inputs to z-scores and p-values — this function does not compute those itself.

import polars as pl


def compute_delta_stats(df):
    mean_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.endswith("_mean_cumsum")]

    # SE = std / sqrt(trials) for every condition
    se_exprs = [
        (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_std_cumsum") / pl.col(f"condition_{i}_trials_cumsum").sqrt())
        .alias(f"condition_{i}_se_cumsum")
        for i in [int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in mean_cols if c.startswith("condition_")]
        if f"condition_{i}_std_cumsum" in df.columns
    ]
    df = df.with_columns(se_exprs)

    # Delta mean and delta SE for each treatment vs condition_0 (control)
    treatment_nums = [
        int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in mean_cols
        if c.startswith("condition_") and int(c.split("_")[1]) > 0
    ]
    delta_exprs = []
    for i in treatment_nums:
        delta_exprs += [
            (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_mean_cumsum") - pl.col("condition_0_mean_cumsum"))
            .alias(f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"),
            (pl.col(f"condition_{i}_se_cumsum").pow(2) + pl.col("condition_0_se_cumsum").pow(2))
            .sqrt()
            .alias(f"condition_{i}_delta_se"),
        ]
    return df.with_columns(delta_exprs)

compute_test_stats converts delta statistics into probabilities and expected values using the normal CDF. For each treatment: Pr(treatment > control) = Φ(delta_mean / delta_se). Expected gain and loss use truncated normal formulas. These are the columns the stopping rule reads — it stops when Pr(Best) crosses the threshold α.

import numpy as np
import polars as pl
from scipy.stats import norm


def compute_test_stats(df):
    delta_mean_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c.endswith("_delta_mean")]
    condition_nums  = [int(c.split("_")[1]) for c in delta_mean_cols]

    pr_cols = []
    for i in condition_nums:
        z = df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() / df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy()
        pr = norm.cdf(z)
        df = df.with_columns([
            pl.lit(pr).alias(f"condition_{i}_pr_positive_diff"),
            pl.lit(1 - pr).alias(f"condition_{i}_pr_lowest_diff"),
        ])
        pr_cols.append(f"condition_{i}_pr_positive_diff")

        # Expected gain and loss (truncated normal)
        pdf_z = norm.pdf(z)
        df = df.with_columns([
            pl.lit(np.abs(pr   * (df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() + df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy() * pdf_z / np.maximum(pr,     1e-10)))).alias(f"condition_{i}_expected_gain"),
            pl.lit(np.abs((1-pr) * (-df[f"condition_{i}_delta_mean"].to_numpy() + df[f"condition_{i}_delta_se"].to_numpy() * pdf_z / np.maximum(1-pr, 1e-10)))).alias(f"condition_{i}_expected_loss"),
        ])

    # Control: Pr(best) = 1 - max(Pr(treatment > control))
    if pr_cols:
        df = df.with_columns([
            (1 - pl.max_horizontal(pr_cols)).alias("condition_0_pr_highest_diff"),
            pl.min_horizontal(pr_cols).alias("condition_0_pr_lowest_diff"),
        ])
    return df

::: {.post-meta}

# TL;DR

Significance testing is excellent at one thing: controlling error rates. If minimizing false positives is your goal, p < 0.05 is the right tool. But most product teams aren't in the business of error control — they're in the business of maximizing long-run value. That's a different objective, and it calls for a different stopping rule.

We ran 100,000 simulated experiments across a range of baseline rates, post-test audiences, and effect distributions, scoring each stopping rule by the long-run conversion rate it produced — test traffic plus full post-test rollout. The findings are consistent across all scenarios.

::: {.callout-important appearance="minimal"}
**The findings:**

- **P-hacking is good for business.**
  - Peeking at results early yields higher long-run value than waiting for a fixed sample size.
  - When an experiment ends inconclusive, deploying whoever is *leading* outperforms defaulting to control.
- **The optimal fixed-sample threshold is α = 0.50.** Not 0.05. Not 0.10. Just deploy whichever variant is winning on the last day, every time. Conventional thresholds don't leave a little value on the table — they actively cause you to default to control when the data already points elsewhere.
:::

In all cases, the optimal stopping rule is far more aggressive than conventional practice — and any rule primarily designed to control error rates lands well short of the peak.

# A Simulation Framework

Traditional power analysis starts from assumptions: fix α = 0.05 and β = 0.20, specify a minimum detectable effect, and it hands you a sample size. That workflow has two problems.

**The MDE is a fiction.** Power analysis requires you to commit to a single assumed effect — but effects follow a distribution. Some tests move the needle, most barely register, a few backfire.

**It isn't forward-looking.** Power analysis tells you when to stop — not what the decision is worth once you ship to the full audience.

This framework addresses both. It simulates experiments across the full distribution of realistic effects and scores each stopping rule by the long-term value it produces — not the error rates it controls.

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
ⓘ **Long-term value** = total conversions generated across the full experiment lifecycle — both arms during the test period, plus the winner deployed to the post-test audience — divided by total users. A stopping rule scores higher by picking the true winner more often or by ending the experiment sooner, both of which put more users on the better variant. *Marginal Events* is the same metric expressed as incremental conversions over running no test.
:::

## Simulation Parameters

In order to determine optimal stopping rules, we simulated 100,000 experiments like the one below. Click any parameter to learn more.

```{=html}
<div class="stopping-factors-ui mt-3 mb-4">

  <!-- Buttons with inputs always visible below each -->
  <div class="factor-columns">

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn factor-btn active w-100" data-target="baseline">
        Baseline
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Conversion</label>
        <input type="text" class="form-control" value="10%" disabled>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn factor-btn w-100" data-target="traffic">
        Test Traffic &amp; Split
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <div class="d-flex gap-2">
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Traffic</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="100,000" disabled>
          </div>
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Split</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="50 / 50" disabled>
          </div>
          <div class="flex-fill">
            <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Min / Max Days</label>
            <input type="text" class="form-control" value="7 / 20" disabled>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn factor-btn w-100" data-target="posttest">
        Post-Test Traffic
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs">
        <label class="param-label" style="text-align:center; display:block;">Post-Test Traffic</label>
        <input type="text" class="form-control" value="500,000" disabled>
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="factor-col">
      <button class="btn factor-btn w-100" data-target="effects">
        Expected Effects
      </button>
      <div class="factor-inputs" style="padding: 0.15rem 0.5rem;">
        <div id="itc-density-label" style="font-size:0.72rem; color:#555; text-align:center; line-height:1.2; margin-bottom:1px;"></div>
        <div id="itc-density-chart" style="height:63px; width:100%; margin-top:0;"></div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>

  <!-- Definition box -->
  <div class="factor-content p-3 border rounded mt-3" id="factor-display">
    <div class="factor-panel" id="panel-baseline">
      The pre-experiment conversion rate. Sets the variance of your test statistic
      and determines how much room for improvement exists.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-traffic">
      Total users in the experiment and their control/treatment split.
      The first 7 days are burn-in — no stopping decisions. The experiment runs at most 20 days.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-posttest">
      The population exposed to the winner after the decision is made. More post-test traffic means more value at stake — potentially justifying a more aggressive stopping rule.
    </div>
    <div class="factor-panel d-none" id="panel-effects">
      The realistic lift you expect comes from a <em>distribution</em> of possible effects — not a single number. Here, experiments have an equal chance of being good or bad.
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="mt-3">
  </div>

</div>



<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const simCollapse = document.getElementById('sim-collapse');
  const simHeader   = document.getElementById('sim-accordion-header');
  const simBtn      = document.getElementById('sim-accordion-btn');
  simCollapse.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse',  () => { simHeader.style.borderBottom = 'none'; simHeader.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; simBtn.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of Control'; });
  simCollapse.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse',  () => { simHeader.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; simHeader.style.borderRadius = '6px'; simBtn.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of Control'; });
});
</script>

<script>
(function renderItcDensity() {
  const el = document.getElementById('itc-density-chart');
  if (!el) return;
  if (typeof Plotly === 'undefined' || !window.__itcDensity) { setTimeout(renderItcDensity, 200); return; }
  const d = window.__itcDensity;
  const lbl = document.getElementById('itc-density-label');
  if (lbl) lbl.innerHTML = `N(μ = ${Math.round(d.mean)}, σ = ${d.std.toFixed(1)})`;
  Plotly.newPlot(el, [
    {
      x: d.xL, y: d.yL, customdata: d.sfL,
      fill: 'tozeroy', mode: 'lines', name: '< 100',
      line: { color: '#e45c3a', width: 1.5 },
      fillcolor: 'rgba(228,92,58,0.25)',
      hovertemplate: 'Pr( ITC > %{x:.2f} ) = %{customdata:.2f}%<extra></extra>',
    },
    {
      x: d.xR, y: d.yR, customdata: d.sfR,
      fill: 'tozeroy', mode: 'lines', name: '> 100',
      line: { color: '#1e46a0', width: 1.5 },
      fillcolor: 'rgba(30,70,160,0.2)',
      hovertemplate: 'Pr( ITC > %{x:.2f} ) = %{customdata:.2f}%<extra></extra>',
    },
    {
      x: [d.mean], y: [0], mode: 'markers', name: 'avg',
      marker: { color: '#1e46a0', size: 7, symbol: 'circle', line: { color: 'white', width: 1.5 } },
      hovertemplate: 'Avg ITC: %{x:.3f}<extra></extra>',
    },
  ], {
    margin: { t: 0, b: 14, l: 2, r: 2 },
    paper_bgcolor: 'rgba(0,0,0,0)',
    plot_bgcolor:  'rgba(0,0,0,0)',
    xaxis: { title: { text: 'Index-to-Control', font: { size: 10 } }, showticklabels: false, showgrid: false, zeroline: false, fixedrange: true },
    yaxis: { visible: false, fixedrange: true },
    showlegend: false,
    shapes: [{ type: 'line', x0: 100, x1: 100, y0: 0, y1: 1, yref: 'paper', line: { color: '#555', width: 1, dash: 'dot' } }],
    hoverlabel: { bgcolor: '#111', font: { color: '#fff', size: 11 } },
  }, { displayModeBar: false, responsive: true });
})();
</script>

<script>
document.querySelectorAll('.factor-btn').forEach(btn => {
  btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    document.querySelectorAll('.factor-btn').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
    document.querySelectorAll('.factor-panel').forEach(p => p.classList.add('d-none'));
    btn.classList.add('active');
    document.getElementById('panel-' + btn.dataset.target).classList.remove('d-none');
  });
});
</script>
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Experiment Assumptions"

import simulationengine as se

# ── Experiment Assumptions ─────────────────────────────────────────
CONTROL_MEAN       = 0.10      # pre-experiment conversion rate
MAX_TEST_N         = 100_000   # total users in the experiment
SPLIT              = 0.50      # treatment allocation (50/50)
MIN_TEST_DAYS      = 7         # mandatory burn-in before stopping decisions
NUMBER_TEST_DAYS   = 20        # maximum experiment duration
POST_TEST_N        = 500_000   # users exposed to winner once MAX_TEST_N is reached
NUMBER_SIMULATIONS = 100_000   # simulations to run
SEED               = 42
ITC_MEAN           = 100       # index-to-control mean
ITC_SE             = 1         # index-to-control standard error

df = se.create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    number_simulations=NUMBER_SIMULATIONS,
    seed=SEED,
    condition_parameters=[
        {
            "mean": CONTROL_MEAN,
            "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
        },
    ],
)

random_effect_parameters_0 = {
    "itc_mean": ITC_MEAN,
    "itc_se": ITC_SE,
    "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
}

df = se.add_random_effect_condition(df, random_effect_parameters_0)
df = se.add_cumulative_columns(df)
df = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(df)
df = se.compute_delta_stats(df)
df = se.compute_test_stats(df)
df = se.calculate_remaining_test_potential(df)
df = se.calculate_launch_potential(df, post_test_n=POST_TEST_N)
df = se.calculate_total_potential(df)

```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="sim-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="sim-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="sim-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#sim-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Show Random Walks of Control</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation is a different realization of the same baseline, but different noise. This is the randomness inherent in experimentation.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="sim-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Simulation Charts"

import json
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import numpy as np
import polars as pl
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
from IPython.display import display as ipy_display

# ── Time series data (first 100 sims, cumulative conversion rate) ─
N_SIMS = 100
plot_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("simulation") <= N_SIMS)
    .sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_0_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)

# ── Serialize all 100 sims for JS slider ─────────────────────────
sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = plot_df.filter(plot_df["simulation"] == sim)
    sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x": sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "y": [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["conversion_rate"].to_list()],
    }

# ── Pre-compute histogram bins for all 10k sims × 20 days ────────
all_df = (
    df.sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_0_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)
hist_data = {}
global_min = float(all_df["conversion_rate"].min())
global_max = float(all_df["conversion_rate"].max())

for day in range(1, NUMBER_TEST_DAYS + 1):
    rates = all_df.filter(all_df["day"] == day)["conversion_rate"].to_numpy()
    counts, edges = np.histogram(rates, bins=50)
    bin_centers = [float((edges[i] + edges[i+1]) / 2) for i in range(len(counts))]
    hist_data[str(day)] = {"x": bin_centers, "y": counts.tolist()}

# ── Subplot: time series (left) + histogram (right) ──────────────
fig = make_subplots(
    cols=2,
    column_widths=[0.5, 0.5],
    horizontal_spacing=0.08,
)

# Manually add left-aligned titles
fig.add_annotation(
    text="Each simulation takes its own path!",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.0, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)
fig.add_annotation(
    text="Control Estimates Improve Over Time (Day = 1)",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.54, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)

# Single trace — JS swaps x/y on slider change (trace index 0)
s1 = plot_df.filter(plot_df["simulation"] == 1)
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1["day"].to_list(),
    y=s1["conversion_rate"].to_list(),
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.9)", width=3),
    opacity=1.0,
    showlegend=False,
    name="Sim 1",
    hovertemplate="%{fullData.name} · %{y:.2%}<extra></extra>",
), col=1, row=1)

fig.add_hline(
    y=CONTROL_MEAN, line_dash="dash",
    line_color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)",
    annotation_text=f"True mean ({CONTROL_MEAN:.0%})",
    annotation_position="bottom right",
    col=1, row=1,
)

d1 = hist_data["1"]
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=d1["x"], y=d1["y"],
    marker_color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.55)",
    showlegend=False,
    hovertemplate="Rate: %{x:.2%}<br>Count: %{y}<extra></extra>",
), col=2, row=1)

fig.add_vline(
    x=CONTROL_MEAN, line_dash="dash",
    line_color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)",
    annotation_text=f"{CONTROL_MEAN:.0%}",
    annotation_position="top right",
    col=2, row=1,
)

fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=300,
    autosize=True,
    margin=dict(t=45, b=30, l=50, r=20),
    hovermode="closest",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="white", font_size=12, namelength=0),
)
fig.update_xaxes(tickformat="d", title_text="Day", col=1, row=1)
fig.update_xaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                 range=[global_min, global_max], col=2, row=1)
ts_min = float(plot_df["conversion_rate"].min())
ts_max = float(plot_df["conversion_rate"].max())
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                 range=[ts_min, ts_max], col=1, row=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="", showticklabels=False, col=2, row=1)

fig

```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(f'<script>window.__histData = {json.dumps(hist_data)}; window.__simData = {json.dumps(sim_data)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">

  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>

  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Day: <span id="day-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="day-slider" type="range" min="1" max="20" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>

</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attach() {
    const plot = document.querySelector('#sim-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!plot || !plot._fullData) { setTimeout(attach, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(plot);

    const simSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
    const simLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
    const daySlider = document.getElementById('day-slider');
    const dayLabel  = document.getElementById('day-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__simData[String(simNum)];
      Plotly.restyle(plot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y], name: [`Sim ${simNum}`] }, [0]);
    }

    simSlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      simLabel.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const tSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
      const tLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');
      if (tSlider && tSlider.value !== this.value) {
        tSlider.value = this.value;
        if (tLabel) tLabel.textContent = this.value;
        tSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const pvSlider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
      const pvLabel  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');
      if (pvSlider && pvSlider.value !== this.value) {
        pvSlider.value = this.value;
        if (pvLabel) pvLabel.textContent = this.value;
        pvSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    daySlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      const day = this.value;
      dayLabel.textContent = day;
      const d = window.__histData[day];
      Plotly.restyle(plot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y] }, [1]);
      Plotly.relayout(plot, { 'annotations[1].text': `Control Estimates Improve Over Time (Day = ${day})` });
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const collapse = document.getElementById('sim-collapse');
  if (collapse) {
    collapse.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attach);
  } else {
    attach();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="treatment-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="treatment-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="treatment-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#treatment-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Show Random Walks of Treatment</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation of treatment is encoded with a different true effect. Sometimes harmful, sometimes beneficial, often marginal. In practice, the true effect is never known.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="treatment-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
# ── Treatment time series (first 100 sims) ───────────────────────
treat_plot_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("simulation") <= N_SIMS)
    .sort(["simulation", "day"])
    .rename({"condition_1_mean_cumsum": "conversion_rate"})
)

treat_true_mean = float(df["condition_1_true_mean"].mean())

treat_sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == sim)
    treat_sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x": sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "y": [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["conversion_rate"].to_list()],
        "true_mean": round(float(sim_df["condition_1_true_mean"].mean()), 6),
    }

# ── ITC histogram (last day, one value per simulation) ────────────
itc_df = (
    df.filter(pl.col("day") == NUMBER_TEST_DAYS)
    .with_columns(
        (100 * pl.col("condition_1_true_mean") / pl.col("condition_0_true_mean")).alias("itc")
    )
    .sort("simulation")
)
itc_values = itc_df["itc"].to_numpy()
itc_counts, itc_edges = np.histogram(itc_values, bins=50)
itc_centers = [float((itc_edges[i] + itc_edges[i+1]) / 2) for i in range(len(itc_counts))]
itc_hist = {"x": itc_centers, "y": itc_counts.tolist()}
itc_max_count = int(itc_counts.max())

# ── KDE density + survival function for ITC mini-chart ───────────
from scipy.stats import gaussian_kde as _kde, norm as _norm
_kde_fn   = _kde(itc_values)
_x_dense  = np.linspace(float(itc_values.min()), float(itc_values.max()), 400)
_y_dense  = _kde_fn(_x_dense)
_itc_mean = float(itc_values.mean())
_itc_std  = float(itc_values.std())
_sf       = [round(float(_norm.sf(x, _itc_mean, _itc_std)) * 100, 2) for x in _x_dense]
_split    = _x_dense <= 100
itc_density = {
    "xL": _x_dense[_split].tolist(),  "yL": _y_dense[_split].tolist(),
    "sfL": [_sf[i] for i in range(len(_x_dense)) if _split[i]],
    "xR": _x_dense[~_split].tolist(), "yR": _y_dense[~_split].tolist(),
    "sfR": [_sf[i] for i in range(len(_x_dense)) if not _split[i]],
    "mean": _itc_mean,
    "std": _itc_std,
}

itc_max_dev = max(abs(float(itc_values.max()) - 100), abs(100 - float(itc_values.min())))
itc_range = [100 - itc_max_dev * 1.1, 100 + itc_max_dev * 1.1]

treat_itc_per_sim = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    rows = itc_df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == sim)["itc"]
    treat_itc_per_sim[str(sim)] = round(float(rows[0]), 4)

# ── Subplot figure ────────────────────────────────────────────────
t_fig = make_subplots(cols=2, column_widths=[0.5, 0.5], horizontal_spacing=0.08)

ts1 = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == 1)
s1_true_mean = round(float(ts1["condition_1_true_mean"].mean()), 6)

t_fig.add_annotation(
    text="Each simulation has a different true mean!",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.0, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)
t_fig.add_annotation(
    text="Distribution of True Treatment Effects (ITC)",
    xref="paper", yref="paper",
    x=0.54, y=1.07, xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
    showarrow=False, font=dict(size=13),
)

# annotation[2]: per-sim true effect label on time series line
t_fig.add_annotation(
    x=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    y=s1_true_mean,
    xref="x1", yref="y1",
    text=f"True mean ({s1_true_mean:.2%})",
    showarrow=False,
    xanchor="right",
    yanchor="top",
    font=dict(size=12, color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)"),
)

ts1 = treat_plot_df.filter(treat_plot_df["simulation"] == 1)

t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=ts1["day"].to_list(),
    y=ts1["conversion_rate"].to_list(),
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.9)", width=3),
    opacity=1.0,
    showlegend=False,
    name="Sim 1",
    hovertemplate="%{fullData.name} · %{y:.2%}<extra></extra>",
), col=1, row=1)

# Trace 2: per-sim true mean line on time series
t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[1, NUMBER_TEST_DAYS],
    y=[s1_true_mean, s1_true_mean],
    mode="lines",
    line=dict(color="rgba(20, 60, 160, 0.8)", width=1.5, dash="dash"),
    showlegend=False,
    hoverinfo="skip",
    name="True Mean",
), col=1, row=1)

# Trace 2: ITC histogram (fixed across all sims)
t_fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=itc_hist["x"], y=itc_hist["y"],
    marker_color="rgba(15, 70, 160, 0.45)",
    showlegend=False,
    hovertemplate="ITC: %{x:.1f}<br>Count: %{y}<extra></extra>",
), col=2, row=1)

# annotation[3]: ITC value label above the green dot
s1_itc = treat_itc_per_sim["1"]
s1_color = "green" if s1_itc >= 100 else "red"
t_fig.add_annotation(
    x=s1_itc,
    y=itc_max_count * 0.1,
    xref="x2", yref="y2",
    text=f"True Effect: {s1_itc:.2f}",
    showarrow=True,
    arrowhead=2,
    arrowsize=2,
    arrowwidth=2,
    arrowcolor=s1_color,
    ax=0, ay=-40,
    font=dict(size=14, color=s1_color),
    bgcolor="white",
    bordercolor=s1_color,
    borderwidth=1,
)
t_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=[s1_itc],
    y=[0],
    mode="markers",
    marker=dict(color=s1_color, size=12, symbol="circle"),
    showlegend=False,
    hoverinfo="skip",
    name="Selected Sim",
), col=2, row=1)

t_fig.update_layout(
    template="plotly_white",
    height=300,
    autosize=True,
    margin=dict(t=45, b=30, l=50, r=20),
    hovermode="closest",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="white", font_size=12, namelength=0),
)
t_fig.update_xaxes(tickformat="d", title_text="Day", col=1, row=1)
t_fig.update_xaxes(tickformat=".1f", title_text="Index-to-Control (ITC)",
                   range=itc_range, col=2, row=1)
t_treat_min = float(treat_plot_df["conversion_rate"].min())
t_treat_max = float(treat_plot_df["conversion_rate"].max())
t_fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".1%", title_text="Conversion Rate",
                   range=[t_treat_min, t_treat_max], col=1, row=1)
t_fig.update_yaxes(title_text="", showticklabels=False, col=2, row=1)

t_fig
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(f'<script>window.__treatSimData = {json.dumps(treat_sim_data)}; window.__treatItcPerSim = {json.dumps(treat_itc_per_sim)}; window.__itcDensity = {json.dumps(itc_density)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">
  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="t-sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="t-sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attachTreatment() {
    const tplot = document.querySelector('#treatment-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!tplot || !tplot._fullData) { setTimeout(attachTreatment, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(tplot);

    const simSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
    const simLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__treatSimData[String(simNum)];
      const itc = window.__treatItcPerSim[String(simNum)];
      const pct = (d.true_mean * 100).toFixed(2) + '%';
      const color = itc >= 100 ? 'green' : 'red';
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.y], name: [`Sim ${simNum}`] }, [0]);
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { y: [[d.true_mean, d.true_mean]] }, [1]);
      Plotly.restyle(tplot, { x: [[itc]], 'marker.color': [color] }, [3]);
      Plotly.relayout(tplot, {
        'annotations[2].y': d.true_mean,
        'annotations[2].text': `True mean (${pct})`,
        'annotations[3].x': itc,
        'annotations[3].text': `True Effect: ${itc.toFixed(2)}`,
        'annotations[3].font.color': color,
        'annotations[3].arrowcolor': color,
        'annotations[3].bordercolor': color,
      });
    }

    simSlider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      simLabel.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const cSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
      const cLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
      if (cSlider && cSlider.value !== this.value) {
        cSlider.value = this.value;
        if (cLabel) cLabel.textContent = this.value;
        cSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const pvSlider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
      const pvLabel  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');
      if (pvSlider && pvSlider.value !== this.value) {
        pvSlider.value = this.value;
        if (pvLabel) pvLabel.textContent = this.value;
        pvSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const tc = document.getElementById('treatment-collapse');
  if (tc) {
    tc.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attachTreatment);
  } else {
    attachTreatment();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  const tc = document.getElementById('treatment-collapse');
  const th = document.getElementById('treatment-accordion-header');
  const tb = document.getElementById('treatment-accordion-btn');
  tc.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse', () => { th.style.borderBottom = 'none'; th.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; tb.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of Treatment'; });
  tc.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse', () => { th.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; th.style.borderRadius = '6px'; tb.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of Treatment'; });
});
</script>
```

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2" id="pvalue-accordion-wrap">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0;" id="pvalue-accordion-header">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              id="pvalue-accordion-btn"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; min-height:0; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse"
              data-bs-target="#pvalue-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Show Random Walks of Probabilities</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">See how Pr(Best) evolves for each condition over time — the signal the stopping rule acts on.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="pvalue-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff;">
```

```{python}
#| echo: false

s1_pv_df = df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == 1).sort("day")

pv_fig = go.Figure()

pv_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1_pv_df["day"].to_list(),
    y=[round(v, 6) for v in s1_pv_df["condition_1_pr_positive_diff"].to_list()],
    mode="lines",
    name="Treatment",
    line=dict(color="#e45c3a", width=2),
    hovertemplate="Pr(Best) = %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    hoverlabel=dict(bgcolor="#c03a1e", font_color="white", bordercolor="#c03a1e", font_size=14),
))
pv_fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=s1_pv_df["day"].to_list(),
    y=[round(v, 6) for v in s1_pv_df["condition_0_pr_highest_diff"].to_list()],
    mode="lines",
    name="Control",
    line=dict(color="#1e46a0", width=2),
    hovertemplate="Pr(Best) = %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>",
    hoverlabel=dict(font_size=14),
))
pv_fig.update_layout(
    height=320,
    yaxis=dict(range=[0, 1.05], title="Pr(Best)", tickformat=".0%"),
    xaxis=dict(title="Day"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.01, xanchor="left", x=0),
    margin=dict(t=40, b=40, l=60, r=80),
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
pv_fig.update_yaxes(gridcolor="#eeeeee")
pv_fig.update_xaxes(gridcolor="#eeeeee")

pv_fig
```

```{python}
#| output: asis

pval_sim_data = {}
for sim in range(1, N_SIMS + 1):
    sim_df = df.filter(pl.col("simulation") == sim).sort("day")
    pval_sim_data[str(sim)] = {
        "x":       sim_df["day"].to_list(),
        "treat":   [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["condition_1_pr_positive_diff"].to_list()],
        "control": [round(v, 6) for v in sim_df["condition_0_pr_highest_diff"].to_list()],
    }

print(f'<script>window.__pvalSimData = {json.dumps(pval_sim_data)};</script>')
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0; display: flex; gap: 2rem; flex-wrap: wrap;">
  <div style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:0.75rem; flex:1; min-width:200px;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:600; white-space:nowrap;">
      Simulation: <span id="pval-sim-label">1</span>
    </label>
    <input id="pval-sim-slider" type="range" min="1" max="100" step="1" value="1"
           style="flex:1; accent-color:#1e46a0;">
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  function attachPval() {
    const pvplot = document.querySelector('#pvalue-collapse .plotly-graph-div');
    if (!pvplot || !pvplot._fullData) { setTimeout(attachPval, 300); return; }

    Plotly.Plots.resize(pvplot);

    const slider = document.getElementById('pval-sim-slider');
    const label  = document.getElementById('pval-sim-label');

    function showSim(idx) {
      const simNum = idx + 1;
      const d = window.__pvalSimData[String(simNum)];
      Plotly.restyle(pvplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.treat]   }, [0]);
      Plotly.restyle(pvplot, { x: [d.x], y: [d.control] }, [1]);
    }

    slider.addEventListener('input', function() {
      label.textContent = this.value;
      showSim(parseInt(this.value) - 1);
      const cSlider = document.getElementById('sim-slider');
      const cLabel  = document.getElementById('sim-label');
      if (cSlider && cSlider.value !== this.value) {
        cSlider.value = this.value;
        if (cLabel) cLabel.textContent = this.value;
        cSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
      const tSlider = document.getElementById('t-sim-slider');
      const tLabel  = document.getElementById('t-sim-label');
      if (tSlider && tSlider.value !== this.value) {
        tSlider.value = this.value;
        if (tLabel) tLabel.textContent = this.value;
        tSlider.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      }
    });

    showSim(0);
  }

  const pc2 = document.getElementById('pvalue-collapse');
  if (pc2) {
    pc2.addEventListener('shown.bs.collapse', attachPval);
    const ph = document.getElementById('pvalue-accordion-header');
    const pb2 = document.getElementById('pvalue-accordion-btn');
    pc2.addEventListener('show.bs.collapse', () => { ph.style.borderBottom = 'none'; ph.style.borderRadius = '6px 6px 0 0'; pb2.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Hide Random Walks of Probabilities'; });
    pc2.addEventListener('hide.bs.collapse', () => { ph.style.borderBottom = '1.5px dashed #90b8e0'; ph.style.borderRadius = '6px'; pb2.querySelector('span:first-child').textContent = 'Show Random Walks of Probabilities'; });
  } else {
    attachPval();
  }
})();
</script>

</div>
</div>
```


## Optimal Stopping Algorithm

To find the stopping rule that maximizes long-term value, we naively apply three analysis strategies and measure their average outcome across the same 100,000 simulated experiments.

| | **Peeking** | **Peeking + Best** | **Fixed Sample** |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Stop early on signal | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| No-breach fallback | Retain Control | Best Performer | Retain Control |

For each of 100,000 simulated experiments and each threshold α ∈ [0.01, 0.50]:

1. **Burn-in:** No stopping decisions before `min_test_days = 7`
2. **Daily check:** Stop when either condition's Pr(Best) exceeds `1 - α`\*
3. **Rollout:** Deploy the winner to remaining test users and post-test traffic
4. **Evaluate:** Pick the α with the highest average long-term outcome

\* *Peeking strategies only. Fixed Sample skips the daily check and always runs to the final day.*

## Results

Below are the optimization curves for each strategy — long-term value as a function of stopping threshold α. The square marker is the peak.

- **Value vs α:** Shows where each strategy is optimized. Compare peak heights to see which strategy produces more value; compare peak positions to see how aggressive the optimal threshold is.
- **Value vs Avg Trials:** Shows the efficiency trade-off. A strategy that reaches the same value with fewer trials is strictly better — it frees up traffic sooner.

```{python}
#| echo: false

import time
import plotly.express as px
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots
from simulationengine import prepare_stopping_decision_columns, evaluate_probability_thresholds

p_thresholds = np.arange(0.5, 1.00, 0.01)  # probabilities, not p-values
normalizer   = df["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + POST_TEST_N
DAILY_N      = MAX_TEST_N / NUMBER_TEST_DAYS  # users per day across all conditions

df_prepped = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    df.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
)
df_prepped = df_prepped.sort(["simulation", "day"])

df_prepped_fixed = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    df.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
)
df_prepped_fixed = df_prepped_fixed.sort(["simulation", "day"])

def run_loop(select_best_at_end, prepped_df=None):
    if prepped_df is None:
        prepped_df = df_prepped
    r = evaluate_probability_thresholds(
        prepped_df,
        threshold_values=p_thresholds,
        metric_direction_improvement="increase",
        pre_sorted=True,
        select_best_at_end=select_best_at_end,
    )
    outcomes   = r["mean_potential_value"].to_list()
    trials     = r["mean_trials"].to_list()
    med_trials = r["median_trials"].to_list()
    rate       = r["match_rate"].to_list()
    avg_days   = (r["mean_trials"] / DAILY_N).to_list()
    return outcomes, trials, med_trials, rate, avg_days

_t0 = time.time()
all_potential_outcomes, total_trials, median_trials, match_rate, all_avg_days = run_loop(True)
ctrl_potential_outcomes, ctrl_trials, ctrl_med_trials, ctrl_match_rate, ctrl_avg_days = run_loop(False)
fixed_potential_outcomes, fixed_trials, fixed_med_trials, fixed_match_rate, fixed_avg_days = run_loop(False, df_prepped_fixed)
_runtime = time.time() - _t0

def make_curve_fig(outcomes, trials, med_trials, rate, title, y_fmt=".3%", round_peak=False):
    y = np.array(outcomes) / normalizer
    x = 1 - p_thresholds
    peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if round_peak else np.argmax(y))
    marginal = (y - CONTROL_MEAN) * (POST_TEST_N + MAX_TEST_N)
    cd = np.column_stack([np.array(trials).astype(int), np.array(rate), np.array(med_trials), marginal])
    hover_tmpl = (
        f"\u03b1: %{{x:.2f}}<br>"
        f"Projected Mean: %{{y:{y_fmt}}}<br>"
        "Marginal Events: %{customdata[3]:,.0f}<br>"
        "Avg Trials: %{customdata[0]:,}<br>"
        "Median Trials: %{customdata[2]:,}<br>"
        "Selected Best Condition: %{customdata[1]:.2%}<br>"
        "<extra></extra>"
    )
    f = px.line(x=x, y=y)
    f.add_scatter(
        x=[x[peak]], y=[y[peak]], mode="markers",
        marker=dict(symbol="square", size=14, color="#3a6fd8"),
        customdata=[[int(trials[peak]), rate[peak], int(med_trials[peak]), marginal[peak]]],
        hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        showlegend=False,
    )
    f.update_traces(selector=dict(mode="lines"), customdata=cd, hovertemplate=hover_tmpl)
    f.update_layout(title_text=title)
    f.update_xaxes(title_text="\u03b1", tickformat=".2f")
    f.update_yaxes(title_text="Projected Mean @ Year-End", tickformat=y_fmt)
    return f

def make_overlay_fig_n(o1, t1, o2, t2, o3, t3, norm):
    """All 3 strategies on one chart: x = avg total trials, y = projected mean."""
    _pal    = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
    _labels = ['Peeking + Best', 'Peeking', 'Fixed Sample']
    _rp     = [False, False, True]
    _x_pvals = 1 - p_thresholds
    fig = go.Figure()
    for _outcomes, _trials, _col, _lbl, _round in zip(
        [o1, o2, o3], [t1, t2, t3], _pal, _labels, _rp
    ):
        _y = np.array(_outcomes) / norm
        _x = np.array(_trials)
        peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(_y * 100, 4)) if _round else np.argmax(_y))
        hover_tmpl = (
            "Avg Trials: %{x:,}<br>"
            "Projected Mean: %{y:.3%}<br>"
            "\u03b1: %{customdata[0]:.2f}<br>"
            "<extra></extra>"
        )
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=_x, y=_y,
            mode='lines+markers',
            name=_lbl,
            line=dict(color=_col, width=2),
            marker=dict(color=_col, size=4),
            customdata=np.column_stack([_x_pvals]),
            hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        ))
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[_x[peak]], y=[_y[peak]], mode='markers', showlegend=False,
            marker=dict(symbol='square', size=10, color=_col),
            customdata=[[_x_pvals[peak]]],
            hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        ))
    fig.update_layout(
        showlegend=True,
        legend=dict(
            orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.2,
            xanchor="center", x=0.5, font=dict(size=10),
        ),
        height=300,
    )
    return fig

def make_triple_curve_fig(o1, t1, m1, r1, o2, t2, m2, r2, o3, t3, m3, r3, norm,
                          baseline=None, total_n=None, y_fmt=".3%"):
    """All 3 strategies overlaid: x = p-value threshold, y = projected mean."""
    _pal    = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
    _labels = ['Peeking + Best', 'Peeking', 'Fixed Sample']
    _rp     = [False, False, True]
    x       = 1 - p_thresholds
    marginal_hover = "Marginal Events: %{customdata[3]:,.0f}<br>" if (baseline is not None and total_n is not None) else ""
    fig = go.Figure()
    for _o, _t, _m, _r, _col, _lbl, _round in zip(
        [o1, o2, o3], [t1, t2, t3], [m1, m2, m3], [r1, r2, r3], _pal, _labels, _rp
    ):
        y = np.array(_o) / norm
        peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if _round else np.argmax(y))
        marginal = (y - baseline) * total_n if (baseline is not None and total_n is not None) else np.zeros(len(y))
        cd = np.column_stack([np.array(_t).astype(int), np.array(_r), np.array(_m), marginal])
        hover_tmpl = (
            f"\u03b1: %{{x:.2f}}<br>"
            f"Projected Mean: %{{y:{y_fmt}}}<br>"
            f"{marginal_hover}"
            "Avg Trials: %{customdata[0]:,}<br>"
            "Selected Best Condition: %{customdata[1]:.2%}<br>"
            "<extra></extra>"
        )
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=x, y=y, mode='lines', name=_lbl,
            line=dict(color=_col, width=2),
            customdata=cd, hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        ))
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
            x=[x[peak]], y=[y[peak]], mode='markers', showlegend=False,
            marker=dict(symbol='square', size=10, color=_col),
            customdata=[[int(_t[peak]), _r[peak], int(_m[peak]), marginal[peak]]],
            hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        ))
    fig.update_xaxes(title_text="\u03b1", tickformat=".2f")
    fig.update_yaxes(title_text="Projected Mean @ Year-End", tickformat=y_fmt)
    fig.update_layout(
        showlegend=True,
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.18, xanchor="center", x=0.5, font=dict(size=10)),
        height=350,
    )
    return fig

def make_scenario_row(o1, t1, m1, r1, o2, t2, m2, r2, o3, t3, m3, r3, norm, baseline, total_n):
    triple  = make_triple_curve_fig(o1, t1, m1, r1, o2, t2, m2, r2, o3, t3, m3, r3, norm, baseline, total_n)
    overlay = make_overlay_fig_n(o1, t1, o2, t2, o3, t3, norm)
    row = make_subplots(rows=1, cols=2,
                        subplot_titles=["Value vs α", "Value vs Avg Trials"],
                        column_widths=[0.5, 0.5],
                        shared_yaxes=True)
    for _tr in triple.data:  row.add_trace(_tr, row=1, col=1)
    for _tr in overlay.data:
        _tr.showlegend = False
        row.add_trace(_tr, row=1, col=2)
    row.update_xaxes(title_text="\u03b1", tickformat=".2f", col=1)
    row.update_yaxes(title_text="Projected Mean @ Year-End", tickformat=".3%", col=1)
    row.update_xaxes(title_text="Avg Total Trials", tickformat=",", col=2)
    row.update_layout(
        showlegend=True,
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="top", y=-0.18, xanchor="center", x=0.5, font=dict(size=10)),
        height=380,
    )
    return row

def make_scenario_stats(o1, d1, t1, r1, o2, d2, t2, r2, o3, d3, t3, r3, norm, baseline, total_n):
    """Returns a list of plain dicts (no HTML) — table is built in JS to avoid Quarto postprocessing."""
    def _peak(outcomes, avg_days, trials, rate, round_peak=False):
        y    = np.array(outcomes) / norm
        x    = 1 - p_thresholds
        peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if round_peak else np.argmax(y))
        return float(x[peak]), float((y[peak] - baseline) * total_n), float(avg_days[peak]), float(y[peak]), float(np.array(rate)[peak]), float(np.array(trials)[peak])
    _pal  = ['#1f77b4', '#ff7f0e', '#2ca02c']
    _lbls = ['Peeking + Best', 'Peeking', 'Fixed Sample']
    _data = [_peak(o1, d1, t1, r1), _peak(o2, d2, t2, r2), _peak(o3, d3, t3, r3, round_peak=True)]
    rows  = [
        {"label": lbl, "color": c, "alpha": a, "marginal": m, "avg_days": d,
         "proj_mean": pm, "best_rate": br, "avg_trials": at, "is_ref": False}
        for (a, m, d, pm, br, at), c, lbl in zip(_data, _pal, _lbls)
    ]
    # Reference row: Fixed Sample at the conventional α = 0.05
    idx_05 = int(np.argmin(np.abs((1 - p_thresholds) - 0.05)))
    y3 = np.array(o3) / norm
    rows.append({
        "label": "Fixed Sample",
        "color": "#2ca02c",
        "alpha": float((1 - p_thresholds)[idx_05]),
        "marginal": float((y3[idx_05] - baseline) * total_n),
        "avg_days": float(np.array(d3)[idx_05]),
        "proj_mean": float(y3[idx_05]),
        "best_rate": float(np.array(r3)[idx_05]),
        "avg_trials": float(np.array(t3)[idx_05]),
        "is_ref": True,
    })
    return rows

row1 = make_scenario_row(
    all_potential_outcomes,   total_trials,    median_trials,    match_rate,
    ctrl_potential_outcomes,  ctrl_trials,     ctrl_med_trials,  ctrl_match_rate,
    fixed_potential_outcomes, fixed_trials,    fixed_med_trials, fixed_match_rate,
    normalizer, CONTROL_MEAN, POST_TEST_N + MAX_TEST_N,
)

from IPython.display import display, HTML
import plotly.io as pio

_stats_base = make_scenario_stats(
    all_potential_outcomes,   all_avg_days,   total_trials,    match_rate,
    ctrl_potential_outcomes,  ctrl_avg_days,  ctrl_trials,     ctrl_match_rate,
    fixed_potential_outcomes, fixed_avg_days, fixed_trials,    fixed_match_rate,
    normalizer, CONTROL_MEAN, POST_TEST_N + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""
<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['500k_s1_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(row1)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_base)}
}};
</script>

<div id="wif-row1" style="min-height:380px;"></div>

<p style="font-size:1.05rem; color:#333; margin-top:0.75rem; margin-bottom:0.25rem;">The table summarizes each strategy at its optimal α, with Fixed Sample at the conventional α = 0.05 included as a benchmark.</p>
<div id="wif-stats1" style="margin-top:0.25rem;"></div>

<div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.6rem; margin-top:0.5rem; margin-bottom:0.15rem; border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius:6px; padding:0.5rem 1rem;">
  <div style="font-size:1.05rem; color:#444; font-style:italic;">Change the scenario — notice whether the peaks shift or hold.</div>
  <div style="display:flex; gap:2rem; align-items:flex-start; flex-wrap:wrap;">
  <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Baseline Conversion</label>
    <select id="wif-cr" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
      <option value="cr05">5%</option>
      <option value="cr10" selected>10%</option>
      <option value="cr30">30%</option>
      <option value="cr65">65%</option>
    </select>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Post-Test N</label>
    <select id="wif-ptn" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
      <option value="100k">100,000</option>
      <option value="250k">250,000</option>
      <option value="500k" selected>500,000</option>
      <option value="2m">2,000,000</option>
    </select>
  </div>
  <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.25rem;">
    <label style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-weight:600;">Expected Effects
      <span id="sigma-info-icon" data-bs-toggle="tooltip" data-bs-placement="top" data-bs-title="Bigger swings result in faster, more accurate experiments — large effects are easier to detect, so the stopping rule fires sooner and picks the right winner more often." style="cursor:help; display:inline-flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; width:14px; height:14px; border-radius:50%; border:1px solid #888; color:#888; font-size:0.65rem; font-weight:700; vertical-align:middle; margin-left:3px;">i</span>
    </label>
    <select id="wif-sigma" class="form-select form-select-sm" style="width:auto; font-size:0.85rem;">
      <option value="s05">ITC ~ N(100, 0.5)</option>
      <option value="s1" selected>ITC ~ N(100, 1)</option>
      <option value="s2">ITC ~ N(100, 2)</option>
    </select>
  </div>
  <div id="wif-context" style="align-self:center; font-size:0.85rem; color:#555; font-style:italic; flex:1; min-width:160px;"></div>
  </div>
</div>

<script>
(function() {{
  var CR_ORDER    = {{ 'cr05': 0, 'cr10': 1, 'cr30': 2, 'cr65': 3 }};
  var PTN_ORDER   = {{ '100k': 0, '250k': 1, '500k': 2, '2m': 3 }};
  var SIGMA_ORDER = {{ 's05': 0, 's1': 1, 's2': 2 }};
  var prevCr    = 'cr10';
  var prevPtn   = '500k';
  var prevSigma = 's1';

  var CR_UP     = 'Higher baseline means larger absolute effects at the same ITC — easier to detect.';
  var CR_DOWN   = 'Lower baseline means smaller absolute effects at the same ITC — harder to detect.';
  var PTN_UP    = 'Larger post-test audience means a bad decision reaches more people — the optimal stopping rule becomes more conservative.';
  var PTN_DOWN  = 'Smaller post-test audience limits the downside of a wrong call — the optimal stopping rule becomes more lenient.';
  var SIGMA_UP   = 'Bigger swings are easier to spot — the experiment ends sooner, accuracy improves, and the value gains are larger.';
  var SIGMA_DOWN = 'Smaller swings are harder to spot — the experiment runs longer, picks the wrong winner more often, and the value gains are smaller.';

  function updateContext(text) {{
    var ctx = document.getElementById('wif-context');
    if (ctx) ctx.textContent = text;
  }}

  document.getElementById('wif-cr').addEventListener('change', function() {{
    var cr = document.getElementById('wif-cr').value;
    if      (CR_ORDER[cr] > CR_ORDER[prevCr]) updateContext(CR_UP);
    else if (CR_ORDER[cr] < CR_ORDER[prevCr]) updateContext(CR_DOWN);
    prevCr = cr;
  }});

  document.getElementById('wif-ptn').addEventListener('change', function() {{
    var ptn = document.getElementById('wif-ptn').value;
    if      (PTN_ORDER[ptn] > PTN_ORDER[prevPtn]) updateContext(PTN_UP);
    else if (PTN_ORDER[ptn] < PTN_ORDER[prevPtn]) updateContext(PTN_DOWN);
    prevPtn = ptn;
  }});

  document.getElementById('wif-sigma').addEventListener('change', function() {{
    var sigma = document.getElementById('wif-sigma').value;
    if      (SIGMA_ORDER[sigma] > SIGMA_ORDER[prevSigma]) updateContext(SIGMA_UP);
    else if (SIGMA_ORDER[sigma] < SIGMA_ORDER[prevSigma]) updateContext(SIGMA_DOWN);
    prevSigma = sigma;
  }});
}})();
</script>

<script>
(function initWif() {{
  if (typeof Plotly === 'undefined') {{ setTimeout(initWif, 200); return; }}

  function renderScenario(key) {{
    var s = window.__wif_scenarios && window.__wif_scenarios[key];
    if (!s) {{ setTimeout(function() {{ renderScenario(key); }}, 200); return; }}
    var r1 = s.row1; r1.layout.height = 380;
    Plotly.react('wif-row1', r1.data, r1.layout);
    var rows = s.stats.map(function(d) {{
      return '<tr data-label="' + d.label + '" data-is-ref="' + d.is_ref + '">' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;font-weight:600;color:' + d.color + ';">' + d.label + '</td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + d.alpha.toFixed(2) + '</strong></td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + (d.proj_mean * 100).toFixed(3) + '%</strong></td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + Math.round(d.marginal).toLocaleString() + '</strong></td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + (d.best_rate * 100).toFixed(1) + '%</strong></td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + Math.round(d.avg_trials).toLocaleString() + '</strong></td>' +
        '<td style="padding:5px 12px;text-align:center;"><strong>' + d.avg_days.toFixed(1) + '</strong></td>' +
        '</tr>';
    }}).join('');
    var _th = 'style="padding:5px 12px;color:#666;font-weight:600;border-bottom:1px solid #ddd;text-align:center;"';
    document.getElementById('wif-stats1').innerHTML =
      '<' + 'table style="width:100%;font-size:0.85rem;border-collapse:collapse;margin-top:0.4rem;">' +
      '<thead><tr>' +
      '<th style="padding:5px 12px;color:#666;font-weight:600;border-bottom:1px solid #ddd;text-align:left;">Strategy</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>\u03b1</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>Projected Mean</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>Marginal Events vs No Test</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>Selected Best Condition</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>Avg Trials</th>' +
      '<th ' + _th + '>Avg(Days)</th>' +
      '</tr></thead><tbody>' + rows + '</tbody></' + 'table>';
  }}

  function getKey() {{
    return document.getElementById('wif-ptn').value + '_' +
           document.getElementById('wif-sigma').value + '_' +
           document.getElementById('wif-cr').value;
  }}

  renderScenario('500k_s1_cr10');
  document.getElementById('wif-ptn').addEventListener('change',   function() {{ renderScenario(getKey()); }});
  document.getElementById('wif-sigma').addEventListener('change', function() {{ renderScenario(getKey()); }});
  document.getElementById('wif-cr').addEventListener('change',    function() {{ renderScenario(getKey()); }});
  var _tip = document.getElementById('sigma-info-icon');
  if (_tip && typeof bootstrap !== 'undefined') {{ new bootstrap.Tooltip(_tip); }}
}})();
</script>
<div style="display:none;">{_runtime:.1f}s</div>
"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 1: N=1M, σ=1

_ptn_2x = POST_TEST_N * 4
df_2x = se.calculate_launch_potential(df, post_test_n=_ptn_2x)
df_2x = se.calculate_total_potential(df_2x)

_norm_2x = df_2x["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_2x

df_prepped_2x = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    df_2x.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

df_fixed_2x = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    df_2x.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

def run_loop_df(df_p, select_best_at_end):
    r = evaluate_probability_thresholds(
        df_p,
        threshold_values=p_thresholds,
        metric_direction_improvement="increase",
        pre_sorted=True,
        select_best_at_end=select_best_at_end,
    )
    outcomes   = r["mean_potential_value"].to_list()
    trials     = r["mean_trials"].to_list()
    med_trials = r["median_trials"].to_list()
    rate       = r["match_rate"].to_list()
    avg_days   = (r["mean_trials"] / DAILY_N).to_list()
    return outcomes, trials, med_trials, rate, avg_days

_o1, _t1, _m1, _r1, _d1 = run_loop_df(df_prepped_2x, True)
_o2, _t2, _m2, _r2, _d2 = run_loop_df(df_prepped_2x, False)
_o3, _t3, _m3, _r3, _d3 = run_loop_df(df_fixed_2x, False)

def make_curve_fig_n(outcomes, trials, med_trials, rate, title, norm, y_fmt=".3%", baseline=None, total_n=None, round_peak=False):
    y = np.array(outcomes) / norm
    x = 1 - p_thresholds
    peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if round_peak else np.argmax(y))
    marginal = (y - baseline) * total_n if (baseline is not None and total_n is not None) else np.zeros(len(y))
    cd = np.column_stack([np.array(trials).astype(int), np.array(rate), np.array(med_trials), marginal])
    marginal_hover = "Marginal Events: %{customdata[3]:,.0f}<br>" if (baseline is not None and total_n is not None) else ""
    hover_tmpl = (
        f"\u03b1: %{{x:.2f}}<br>"
        f"Projected Mean: %{{y:{y_fmt}}}<br>"
        f"{marginal_hover}"
        "Avg Trials: %{customdata[0]:,}<br>"
        "Median Trials: %{customdata[2]:,}<br>"
        "Selected Best Condition: %{customdata[1]:.2%}<br>"
        "<extra></extra>"
    )
    f = px.line(x=x, y=y)
    f.add_scatter(
        x=[x[peak]], y=[y[peak]], mode="markers",
        marker=dict(symbol="square", size=14, color="#3a6fd8"),
        customdata=[[int(trials[peak]), rate[peak], int(med_trials[peak]), marginal[peak]]],
        hovertemplate=hover_tmpl,
        showlegend=False,
    )
    f.update_traces(selector=dict(mode="lines"), customdata=cd, hovertemplate=hover_tmpl)
    f.update_layout(title_text=title)
    f.update_xaxes(title_text="\u03b1", tickformat=".2f")
    f.update_yaxes(title_text="Projected Mean @ Year-End", tickformat=y_fmt)
    return f

_row_2x   = make_scenario_row(
    _o1, _t1, _m1, _r1,
    _o2, _t2, _m2, _r2,
    _o3, _t3, _m3, _r3,
    _norm_2x, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_2x + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_2x = make_scenario_stats(
    _o1, _d1, _t1, _r1, _o2, _d2, _t2, _r2, _o3, _d3, _t3, _r3,
    _norm_2x, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_2x + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['2m_s1_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_2x)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_2x)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
# Scenario 2: N=500k, σ=2

_itc_se_wide = 2
_ptn_wide    = POST_TEST_N   # 500k — effects only
_ref_params_wide = {
    "itc_mean": ITC_MEAN,
    "itc_se": _itc_se_wide,
    "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
}

_df_base = se.create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    number_simulations=NUMBER_SIMULATIONS,
    seed=SEED,
    condition_parameters=[{"mean": CONTROL_MEAN, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}],
)
_df_wide = se.add_random_effect_condition(_df_base, _ref_params_wide)
_df_wide = se.add_cumulative_columns(_df_wide)
_df_wide = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(_df_wide)
_df_wide = se.compute_delta_stats(_df_wide)
_df_wide = se.compute_test_stats(_df_wide)
_df_wide = se.calculate_remaining_test_potential(_df_wide)
_df_wide = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_wide, post_test_n=_ptn_wide)
_df_wide = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_wide)

_norm_wide = _df_wide["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_wide

_df_prepped_wide = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_wide.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_df_fixed_wide = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_wide.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_ow1, _tw1, _mw1, _rw1, _dw1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_wide, True)
_ow2, _tw2, _mw2, _rw2, _dw2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_wide, False)
_ow3, _tw3, _mw3, _rw3, _dw3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_wide, False)

_row_wide   = make_scenario_row(
    _ow1, _tw1, _mw1, _rw1,
    _ow2, _tw2, _mw2, _rw2,
    _ow3, _tw3, _mw3, _rw3,
    _norm_wide, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_wide + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_wide = make_scenario_stats(
    _ow1, _dw1, _tw1, _rw1, _ow2, _dw2, _tw2, _rw2, _ow3, _dw3, _tw3, _rw3,
    _norm_wide, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_wide + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['500k_s2_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_wide)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_wide)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

#| echo: false
# Scenario 3: N=1M, σ=2

_ptn_both    = POST_TEST_N * 4
_itc_se_both = 2
_ref_params_both = {
    "itc_mean": ITC_MEAN,
    "itc_se": _itc_se_both,
    "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT),
}

_df_base_b = se.create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
    number_simulations=NUMBER_SIMULATIONS,
    seed=SEED,
    condition_parameters=[{"mean": CONTROL_MEAN, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}],
)
_df_both = se.add_random_effect_condition(_df_base_b, _ref_params_both)
_df_both = se.add_cumulative_columns(_df_both)
_df_both = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(_df_both)
_df_both = se.compute_delta_stats(_df_both)
_df_both = se.compute_test_stats(_df_both)
_df_both = se.calculate_remaining_test_potential(_df_both)
_df_both = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_both, post_test_n=_ptn_both)
_df_both = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_both)

_norm_both = _df_both["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_both

_df_prepped_both = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_both.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_df_fixed_both = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
    _df_both.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
    metric_direction_improvement="increase",
).sort(["simulation", "day"])

_ob1, _tb1, _mb1, _rb1, _db1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_both, True)
_ob2, _tb2, _mb2, _rb2, _db2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_both, False)
_ob3, _tb3, _mb3, _rb3, _db3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_both, False)

_row_both   = make_scenario_row(
    _ob1, _tb1, _mb1, _rb1,
    _ob2, _tb2, _mb2, _rb2,
    _ob3, _tb3, _mb3, _rb3,
    _norm_both, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_both + MAX_TEST_N,
)
_stats_both = make_scenario_stats(
    _ob1, _db1, _tb1, _rb1, _ob2, _db2, _tb2, _rb2, _ob3, _db3, _tb3, _rb3,
    _norm_both, CONTROL_MEAN, _ptn_both + MAX_TEST_N,
)

display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['2m_s2_cr10'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_both)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_both)}
}};
</script>"""))
